mining-laser-flora.name: Mining Laser on flora
mining-laser-flora.detail: Aim the Mining Laser (multi-tool) at trees, bushes, or vegetation. Each plant drops whatever element it's tagged with on the Analysis Visor — element types vary by planet biome, so scan a new plant before harvesting to confirm the yield.

hazardous-flora.name: Destroying hazardous plants
hazardous-flora.detail: On hostile biomes you'll see spiky, knotty, or bladed defensive plants. Shoot them (or strike with the Mining Laser) to destroy them — each one drops a single burst of whatever element matches the planet's environment. They retaliate, so have shields or keep moving.

surface-minerals.name: Surface mineral deposits
surface-minerals.detail: Large scanner-tagged rocks sitting on a planet's surface. Use the Analysis Visor to highlight them from a distance. The Mining Laser works for small nodes; switch to the Terrain Manipulator on deep earth deposits to pull out much larger yields in one go.

crystal-deposits.name: Crystal formations
crystal-deposits.detail: Colored crystalline spires and clusters that grow on planet surfaces. Different crystal colors produce different elements — use the Analysis Visor to identify a crystal type before mining. Harvest with the Mining Laser or Terrain Manipulator.

cave-deposits.name: Cave deposits
cave-deposits.detail: Mineral clusters that grow inside caves. Caves hide different elements depending on the planet's conditions; use your scanner once underground to reveal what's available.

underwater-deposits.name: Underwater deposits
underwater-deposits.detail: Ocean-floor mineral deposits and anomalous aquatic growths on water planets. You'll need the Aquajet Pack plus Terrain Manipulator. Scan the seabed — the element varies per planet, but yields tend to be generous once you find a node.

asteroids-space.name: Asteroid mining (space)
asteroids-space.detail: Shoot asteroids with your starship's mining laser while flying through space. Most asteroids are grey and drop common fuel; rarer colored asteroids (gold, silver, platinum) show up in specific star-system types. A great passive source while travelling between planets.

atmosphere-harvester.name: Atmosphere Harvester
atmosphere-harvester.detail: A base-built structure that slowly extracts gas from a planet's atmosphere. The gas type depends on the biome. Requires power. Stack multiple harvesters for serious throughput, and queue recipes before logging off to harvest while you're away.

refining.name: Refining
refining.detail: Process materials in a Portable, Medium, or Large Refiner (1, 2, or 3 inputs respectively). Refining is the backbone of crafting — most refined elements and rare alloys are only obtainable this way. Browse the Recipes tab → Refiner to see every valid input combination.

cooking.name: Nutrient Processor
cooking.detail: A food-specific refiner. Combine flora, fauna products, and processed ingredients to produce dishes. Some cooked items sell for very high prices at trade terminals, others are tied to expedition or milestone rewards.

creature-harvest.name: Creature harvesting
creature-harvest.detail: Feed or bond with creatures on a planet. Wild fauna drop one material when fed (milk, eggs, droppings); adopted companion creatures drop rarer specific products over time. Harvested creature products feed into cooking and certain late-game recipes.

sentinels-defeat.name: Defeating Sentinels
sentinels-defeat.detail: Kill Sentinel drones, Quads, Walkers, and ship variants for their drops. Dissonant / Sentinel-controlled planets have constant patrols making this the most reliable farming ground. Higher-tier enemies drop rarer components.

pirate-loot.name: Pirate freighter raids
pirate-loot.detail: Attack pirate freighters or intercept cargo pods in pirate-controlled systems. Pays out in nanites, contraband, and seasonal smuggler-exclusive items. Freighter raids scale with your combat tech.

expedition-rewards.name: Expeditions
expedition-rewards.detail: Complete Expedition milestones (limited-time seasonal content) for exclusive materials, ships, frigates, and cosmetics. Progress exports back to your main save when the expedition ends.

twisted-worlds.name: Twisted / corrupted worlds
twisted-worlds.detail: Dissonant, cursed, and Atlantid-infused planets added in recent updates. Usually late-game: specific equipment, questlines, or Atlas interactions are required before a planet's rare material becomes accessible.

scrapping-tech.name: Scrapping technology
scrapping-tech.detail: Dismantle starship, multi-tool, or exocraft tech at their respective terminals. Returns a fraction of the build cost plus scrap materials and occasional nanites. Very reliable nanite income for mid-game players.

freighter-salvage.name: Crashed freighter salvage
freighter-salvage.detail: Crashed freighter sites on planet surfaces are marked by distress beacons. Dig up buried storage crates with the Terrain Manipulator — large sites have 4–6 crates plus a central module cache. One of the best sources of rare building/upgrade materials.

farming.name: Hydroponic farming
farming.detail: Plant seeds in Hydroponic Trays indoors or biodomes on the surface. Seeds come from Galactic Trade Terminals or the Space Anomaly. Plants take real time to grow. Once scaled up, farming is the most efficient economic loop in the mid-game.

monolith-rewards.name: Monoliths & ancient sites
monolith-rewards.detail: Interact with Monoliths, Plaques, Ruins, and Ancient Bones on planet surfaces. Correct dialogue picks grant faction standing, language words, occasional materials, and sometimes keys to deeper quests.

raw1.note: On most lush and warm planets, flora drops Carbon. Tall trees yield the most per hit.
On lush/warm planets, hazardous flora also drop Carbon.

raw3.note: Oxygen refines from many common inputs — e.g. Kelp Sac, Cactus Flesh, or organic substances in a Medium Refiner.
On toxic biomes, flora drops Oxygen instead of Carbon.
On toxic biomes, destroying hazardous plants releases a burst of Oxygen each.

raw5.note: Di-hydrogen appears as tall blue crystal spires on virtually every planet. Primary starship launch fuel.
Each asteroid drops a small amount of Di-hydrogen alongside Tritium.

raw7.note: Tritium is the main asteroid-mining yield — the grey common asteroids. Used to power your starship pulse drive.
